title: Accessories
content: Accessories are equipable  items that can provide various stat boosts to the player, including defense , damage , and special abilities (such as limited flight ). Accessories must be placed in a character's accessory slots to work, with the exception of some informational accessories which work simply by being in a character's inventory . Duplicate accessories cannot be equipped, but the Tinkerer's Workshop can be used to craft different accessories together into a new accessory combining their effects and often enhancing them or adding new ones. The five accessory slots (rows) in the inventory, in Classic Mode. The right column is for functional accessories, the central column is for cosmetic accessories, and the left column is for dyes . In Classic Mode worlds, the player is limited to five accessory slots. In Expert Mode (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) , a sixth slot is obtainable by consuming a Demon Heart . Ultimately, Master Mode (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) includes a sixth slot by default which, when combined with a Demon Heart, provides seven slots. The Demon Heart slot and Master Mode slot cannot be used in difficulties lower than their respective ones, including the difficulty set in Journey Mode (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) worlds. Each accessory slot has a respective vanity slot, which allows a character to equip an accessory for purely cosmetic or storage purposes (meaning no stat bonuses), and a dye slot, which changes the color of the accessory. (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) Each accessory slot has a button ( / ) that toggles the visibility of the corresponding accessory. This only affects the character's appearance; the effects of the accessories are still applied. Types Movement Informational Health and Mana Combat Debuff Immunity Construction Fishing Yoyos Miscellaneous Vanity Music Boxes Golf Balls Expert Mode-exclusive Modifiers Main article: Modifier Most accessories (and weapons ) can have modifiers affecting the user's (or weapons') statistics. Most eligible items will get a modifier upon creation, but an item can also gain one later, or have its modifier changed via reforging at the Goblin Tinkerer . Modifiers provide bonuses that are broadly useful for as long as the item is equipped. Accessories can only get bonuses from modifiers, never penalties. Accessory modifiers can provide 1–4 points of defense , 20 points of mana capacity, or a 1%–4% bonus on any of several stats: damage , critical strike chance , movement speed , or melee speed . Modifier bonuses stack freely. Notes Although it is possible to equip an accessory and its materials at the same time, such as the Spectre Boots and Hermes Boots , their effects will not necessarily combine. Stat-boost items are more likely to stack with their own components (for example, the various Emblems), but this is not universal. Check the items' respective pages for which accessories can stack their effects. Informational accessories do not have to be worn in accessory slots. They will still provide information if they are in the player's inventory or Void Bag , but their modifiers will have no effect. PDA and all its components can also be placed in social slots without losing their functions. They can also share their info with players of the same team in multiplayer that are within 50 tiles. Equipping an accessory which provides immunity to a certain debuff will also remove the debuff if the player is afflicted with it. When multiple accessories that are visible on the same location are worn, such as a Panic Necklace and a Cross Necklace , the accessory on the lowest vanity slot will be visible. If no vanity accessories are worn, the accessory on the lowest equipment slot will be shown. Accessories that show up on the head, such as a Nature's Gift or Diving Gear , will not show up if a helmet is worn, unless a Familiar Wig is worn over it. Dyes will affect Wings even if they are not normally visible. Accessories that have no appearance when worn can still be placed in the vanity slots. Most shields , a type of accessory, can synergize with the Brand of the Inferno (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) to block attacks, granting the Striking Moment buff. Tips If spare vanity accessory slots are available, it is useful to put accessories in them as backup ( autopause is helpful for this). For example, the Megaphone can be placed in the vanity slot; if the player gets afflicted with the Silenced debuff , the Megaphone can be swapped in to clear it. This also makes it easier to swap in swimming buffs when traversing water. Alternatively, loadouts (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) can be used for this purpose. See also Guide:Crafting quick reference - Shows complex accessory crafting trees. History Desktop version Desktop 1.4.4 : Reverted the change made in 1.4.0.1 where only accessories with a visual vanity effect could be placed in vanity accessory slots. All accessories can now go in those slots again. Desktop 1.4.1 : Remade the way accessories are displayed to allow for many accessories at once, especially on the character's back (e.g. now possible to show both wings and a cape at the same time). Desktop 1.4.0.1 : Added an additional accessory slot to players in Master Mode that can be used alongside the additional slot from the Demon Heart . Vanity Accessory slots now can only hold accessories with a visual vanity effect. Desktop 1.3.0.1 : New accessories added. Desktop 1.2.4 : Adding Ginger Beard , Frog Leg , High Test Fishing Line , Angler Earring , and Tackle Box . Fixed bugs/glitches related to infinite Hoverboard hovering time, issue with dye -ing the Flying Carpet , wings + Merfolk incompatibility, and Titan Glove + its upgrades not granting melee KB only. Desktop 1.2.3.1 : Due to layering issues, the player can no longer show a shield and a cloak at the same time. Fixed bug where hidden accessories still applied their dye color (wings still apply it if hidden but in the air). The player can only equip one pair of wings at a time. Face accessories that should not show hair no longer show hair. Desktop 1.2.3 : Accessories are now visible on the player. Added 3 new accessories. Visibility can be toggled. Added vanity & dye slots for accessories. Fixed bug where info accessories displayed info when the player looked at empty signs but not when they looked at texty signs. Desktop 1.2.2 : Added 2 new accessories. Desktop 1.2.1.2 : Added 8 new accessories. Critical Strike increase on accessory reforges have been doubled. Tabi and Master Ninja Gear now bring the player to full speed instead of stopping. Desktop 1.2 : New accessories added. Desktop 1.1 : Accessories can now spawn with a modifier that adds an additional benefit. See the Modifier page for more details. Desktop 1.0.6 : It is now impossible to equip more than one of the same accessory at a time. Note: This effect isn't retroactive and characters made before the update that were previously wearing multiple copies of the same accessory will still have them equipped.

title: Combat Accessories
content: Main article: Accessories ↑ Werewolf and Merfolk forms are buffs that affect character appearance. They are only active if the required conditions are met. ↑ The Frozen Turtle Shell provides a visual effect if the wearer is below 25% remaining health. ↑ Obsidian Rose is only visible when no helmet or vanity headpiece is equipped (unless it is one that does not hide the player's head, such as the Familiar Wig ).

title: Faeling
content: Desktop / Console / Mobile -Only Content : This  information applies only to the Desktop , Console , and Mobile versions of Terraria . The Faeling is a flying critter that emits light, which naturally spawns in the Aether . Most critters that come into contact with Shimmer will also transform into Faelings. Faelings can pass through blocks and try to fly away from the player if they get too close. The Faeling has only 5 health and no defense , and can be damaged by other enemies and players. The Faeling can be caught with any Bug Net to be carried around in the inventory and released later, or crafted into animated decorative furniture ( Faeling in a Bottle ). Once crafted, the Faeling cannot be released again, unless the furniture item is de-crafted via Shimmer (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) . Crafting Used in Notes If there are no existing Faelings, 2–5 Faelings will spawn immediately when a player enters the Aether, regardless of NPC spawn rates. When a stack of critter items is thrown into Shimmer to spawn Faelings, a maximum of 50 items will be transmuted at a time. The rest of the stack will float upwards and can be picked back up after the transmutation. The Faelings themselves, Party Bunny , Gold Worm , Gem Bunnies , and Gem Squirrels are the only critters that do not transform into Faelings during a Shimmer Transmutation . Gold Worms transmute into Gummy Worms instead. Gem Bunnies and Gem Squirrels are transformed into their respective gems. The walking variants of Goldfish and Gold Goldfish do not turn into Faelings when they are partially submerged in small amounts of Shimmer. However, doing so while it is not raining, or turning them back into their swimming form by any means will have the same effect as most critters. Critters spawned from statues will disappear upon transmutation rather than become Faelings. Trivia The Bestiary entry for the Faeling: " These scintillating fairy-like creatures are born when animals wander into the glowing depths of a Shimmer pond. " History Desktop version Desktop 1.4.4 : Introduced. Console version Console 1.4.4 : Introduced. Nintendo Switch version Switch 1.4.4 : Introduced. Mobile version Mobile 1.4.4 : Introduced. References ↑ Information taken from the Desktop 1.4.4.9 source code, method SpawnFaelings() in Terraria.NPC.cs . There may be inaccuracies, as the current Desktop version is 1.4.5.6. ↑ Information taken from the Desktop 1.4.4.9 source code, method GetShimmered() in Terraria.Item.cs . There may be inaccuracies, as the current Desktop version is 1.4.5.6.

title: Shimmer Slime
content: Desktop / Console / Mobile -Only Content : This  information applies only to the Desktop , Console , and Mobile versions of Terraria . The Shimmer Slime is a pre-Hardmode enemy and a slime variant that cannot be found naturally, and is instead created when most varieties of slimes come into contact with Shimmer or upon being sprayed by the Shimmer Gun (with exceptions below ). Shimmer Slimes have the ability to reflect projectiles, similarly to Shimmer itself. For the complete list, see the list of reflected projectiles . Like most other slimes, the Shimmer Slime will be passive towards any player with the Royal Gel accessory equipped. Notes Lava Slimes , Dungeon Slimes , King Slime , Spiked Slimes , Gastropods , Queen Slime , Crystal Slimes , Bouncy Slimes , Heavenly Slimes , Hoppin' Jacks , Golden Slimes , and statue-spawned Slimes cannot be transformed into a Shimmer Slime. The Shimmer Slime is not required for the Gelatin World Tour achievement. Trivia The Bestiary entry for the Shimmer Slime: " After a quick swim in the Shimmer, this prismatic slime has been infused with the powers of the mysterious fluid. " History Desktop version Desktop 1.4.5.0 : Now drop Gel . Desktop 1.4.4.3 : Now affected by the Royal Gel . Desktop 1.4.4 : Introduced. Console version Console 1.4.4 : Introduced. Nintendo Switch version Switch 1.4.4 : Introduced. Mobile version Mobile 1.4.4 : Introduced. References ↑ Information taken from the Desktop 1.4.4.9 source code, method Load() in Terraria.Initializers.AchievemntInitializer.cs . There may be inaccuracies, as the current Desktop version is 1.4.5.6.
